title: (Pwn2Own) QNAP QHora-322 miro_webserver_controllers_api_login_singIn Authentication Bypass Vulnerability
description:
This vulnerability allows network-adjacent attackers to bypass authentication on affected installations of QNAP QHora-322 routers. Authentication is not required to exploit this vulnerability.
The specific flaw exists within the miro_webserver_controllers_api_login_singIn function. The issue results from allowing a user to authenticate using an arbitrary QCloud account. An attacker can leverage this vulnerability to bypass authentication on the system.
addtionnal_details: QNAP has issued an update to correct this vulnerability. More details can be found at:https://www.qnap.com/en/security-advisory/qsa-25-15
timeline:
2024-12-13 - Vulnerability reported to vendor
2026-03-30 - Coordinated public release of advisory
2026-03-30 - Advisory Updated
credit: nella17 (@nella17tw), working with DEVCORE Internship Program, and DEVCORE Research Team
